message:
fix(aws): accept quoted table identifiers in athena DELETE rules

Review-pass follow-up on ba90709. The table-name regex on both the
safe `athena-delete-with-where` allowlist and the destructive
`athena-query-delete-without-where` block was
`[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_.]*` — which silently skips quoted identifiers
like `"my-table"` or `` `my-table` ``. That meant:

  aws athena start-query-execution \
    --query-string 'DELETE FROM "reporting-events"'

slipped through BOTH patterns and was allowed by default. Real Athena
usage quotes any identifier containing hyphens or reserved words, so
this is a mainline path, not an edge case.

Widen both patterns to `\S+` for the table name. Since the patterns
still anchor on `DELETE\s+FROM\s+`, the only thing `\S+` absorbs is
the identifier up to the next whitespace, which is correct for bare,
schema-qualified, double-quoted, and backtick-quoted forms alike.

Regression test `athena_delete_with_quoted_identifiers_is_still_matched`
covers all four shapes (quote style × WHERE present/absent).
